mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 30e powertrain replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW X3 30e powertrain costs £5,000–£8,500 supply only, varying by generation: F25 N20 £4,500–£7,500, G01 B48 £5,000–£8,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. Battery replacement is a separate cost — approximately £6,000–£10,000.

That depends on which generation. The F25 (2015–2017) uses the N20 engine with its well-documented timing chain guide failure risk — this is the defining issue. The G01 (2017+) uses the B48, a significantly more reliable engine with the chain front-mounted. Both share PHEV battery degradation concerns. The G01 B48 is the more reliable choice.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 30e?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20: timing chain guide failure (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500). B48: cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank, £400–£1,000). Both: PHEV battery degradation (reduced electric range, £6,000–£10,000), and hybrid system software issues (£150–£500).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 30e powertrain, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: X3 30e values range from £12,000–£35,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £5,000–£8,500 reconditioned powertrain represents 15–70% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained G01, replacement is often justified. For an F25 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 30e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 30e has used two powertrains: N20B20 + e-motor (F25, 2015–2017, 245bhp) and B48B20 + e-motor (G01, 2017–2024, 292bhp). Check your VIN or registration year — pre-2017 is N20, 2017+ is B48. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the battery degradation issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: In many cases, individual battery modules can be replaced (£2,000–£4,000 per module) rather than the entire pack (£6,000–£10,000). A BMW specialist can perform a battery health check to determine which cells have degraded. At the first sign of significant range reduction, book a health check. Regular AC charging (rather than rapid DC charging) and avoiding deep discharges help preserve battery life.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X3 30e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Assess battery health; module replacement may be possible
Software update — almost always the fix
Replacement often £5,000–£8,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X3 30e? Check These First
buyIf: * G01 (2017+) with B48 — the more reliable powertrain
* N20 (F25) with documented timing chain history (guides and tensioner replaced)
* Full BMW service history with documented battery health checks
* Recent software updates applied (PCM, BMS, TCU)
* Low-mileage with documented charging history (AC charging preferred)
avoidIf: * Pre-2017 N20 with no timing chain history — the risk is real
* Significant reduction in electric-only range (battery degradation warning)
* Any hybrid system warnings or drive mode malfunctions without a documented fix
* Poor service history — the PHEV system is complex and unforgiving
* Frequent rapid charging history — accelerates battery degradation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 30e is BMW's plug-in hybrid SUV, combining the smooth B48/N20 petrol with electric power. The G01 B48 is the sweet spot — reliable engine, larger battery, and proven hybrid system. The F25 N20 has the chain risk and early hybrid teething issues. Buy the G01, maintain the battery and cooling system, and the 30e will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW X3 30e powertrain prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 30e (or before you commit to a powertrain replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any rattle? N20 chain rattle is the biggest warning; B48 should be quiet
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — F25 = N20, G01 = B48
* Timing Chain History — N20: chains replaced? Tensioner checked? (pre-2015 is critical)
* Battery Health — Check the estimated electric range; any significant drop from claim?
* Charging History — Has the vehicle been charged correctly? Frequent rapid charging can degrade batteries
* Coolant Level — B48: any loss? Water pump and expansion tank are key risks
* Hybrid System — Check all drive modes work; any warning lights or messages?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
